title: hsquared Agent Instructions
text: hsquared R-facing package identity open, Julia-backed quantitative-genetic modelling system. Julia engine lives sibling repository HSquared.jl.

title: hsquared Agent Instructions
text: Keep hsquared applied-user interface: formula syntax, data validation, R-style summaries, extractors, examples, bridge glue. Keep HSquared.jl computational engine: sparse relationship matrices, REML/ML, solvers, EBVs, G matrices, performance work. Phase 0 operating-system honest-scaffold phase. claim model fitting implementation, validation, documentation, check-log evidence exist. first v0.1 target univariate Gaussian animal model:
code: hsquared(y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = TRUE )

title: hsquared Agent Instructions
text: Users gold. public API easy, easy, easy understand. common animal-model path read like model applied user already mind. Error messages name unsupported syntax point closest implemented planned path. R Julia syntax stay transferable whenever engine can honestly support concept. Specialist machinery belongs behind intuitive defaults users ask . Planned genomic, QTL, selfing, clonal, haplodiploid, polyploid, GLLVM-style features must stay visible roadmap without making current API feel crowded. Every Phase 1+ slice include Jason scout pass across local sister repos (drmTMB, gllvmTMB, DRM.jl, GLLVM.jl, nearby statistical packages present) plus literature/package check slice changes scientific design public claims.

title: hsquared Roadmap
text: hsquared HSquared.jl twin packages one project identity. hsquared owns R interface; HSquared.jl owns sparse Julia engine.

what: Phase 0: Team Operating System And Public Scaffold
title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: complete public scaffold. Install repo-visible memory, team roles, local skills, coordination board, claim gates. Keep README/DESCRIPTION wording honest: scaffold, working animal-model software. Coordinate Julia twin thread HSquared.jl. Create labels, milestones, issues Phase 0 docs committed. Gate: repos honest READMEs, CI, team docs, unsupported fitting claims. Julia twin status: HSquared.jl public green GitHub Actions Phase 0 scaffold. R twin status: hsquared public green GitHub Actions commit 2268ff4. Issue ledger: hsquared: issues #1-#7 cover Phase 0 initial Phase 1 R/coordinator work. HSquared.jl: issues #1-#7 cover Phase 0 initial Phase 1 Julia engine work.

title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: started. R formula: animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) parser partial. Julia sparse pedigree parser Ainv. Gaussian ML/REML engine. EBVs/BLUPs, variance components, heritability. First tiny Mrode-style validation examples. Standing Phase 1+ rule: check local sister packages relevant statistical literature changing grammar, engine contracts, validation claims, roadmap promises.

title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: planned. Repeatability. Permanent environment. Maternal common environment. Sire models. Groups unknown parent groups. Inbreeding coefficients. First random-regression slice.

title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: planned. Long-format trait grammar. Full G R matrices. Missing trait records. Genetic correlations cross-trait EBVs.

title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: planned. cov = diag(). cov = lowrank(K). cov = fa(K). Loadings, specific variance, latent breeding values, eigen evolvability tools.

title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: planned. GBLUP, SNP-BLUP, Ginv, Hinv, single-step HBLUP, APY, marker-derived genomic relationships, genomic feature/QTL-style effects, simulation validation.

what: Phase 6: Non-Gaussian And GLLVM Animal Models
title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: planned. Poisson, negative binomial, binomial, beta-binomial, ordinal, zero-inflated hurdle extensions. Wide response matrices, latent genetic axes, ordination, community ecology examples.

title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: planned. Selfing, clonal/asexual, haplodiploid, polyploid, cytoplasmic, dominance, epistasis, custom inheritance kernels.

title: hsquared Roadmap
text: Status: planned. Disk-backed workflows, PCG/preconditioners, GPU-aware dense/factor models, million-record benchmarks.

title: Genomics, QTL, and CPU/GPU roadmap
text: R package keep easy say. Julia package provide performance-oriented engine, speed scale treated benchmarked claims rather slogans.
code: phenotype =   fixed effects   + pedigree/genetic effects   + genomic marker effects   + maternal/paternal effects   + environment/common effects   + latent multivariate factors   + residual variation

title: Genomics, QTL, and CPU/GPU roadmap
text: Marker scan syntax planned later:
code: fit <- hsquared(   y ~ sex + batch + genomic(1 | id, Ginv = Ginv),   data = pheno,   family = gaussian() ) fit <- hsquared(   y ~ sex + age +     genomic(1 | id, Ginv = Ginv) +     marker_scan(M, map = marker_map, leave_one_chr_out = TRUE),   data = pheno,   genotypes = geno,   family = gaussian() )

title: Genomics, QTL, and CPU/GPU roadmap
text: CPU trusted default. GPU accelerator path must benchmarkable numerically checked. Planned Julia backends: CPU threaded CPU; CUDA NVIDIA GPUs; Metal Mac/Apple Silicon testing; AMDGPU ROCm systems; oneAPI Intel accelerators; portable kernels appropriate.

title: Genomics, QTL, and CPU/GPU roadmap
text: package claim ASReml-level performance, GPU speedup, genomic support, QTL/eQTL support, GLLVM support code, tests, docs, validation evidence exist.

title: Getting started with hsquared
text: parser currently accepts: one animal() term; random-intercept syntax: 1 | id; pedigree = ped; numeric Gaussian responses. Unsupported syntax still important defines roadmap. Terms cov = fa(K = 2), genomic(), single_step(), QTL effects, selfing, polyploidy, haplodiploidy, GLLVM-style latent genetic axes planned lanes, current fitting features.

title: Getting started with hsquared
text: intended flow : R owns friendly interface. Julia owns speed, sparse computation, experimental engine work.
code: R formula + data -> R validation and model specification -> R bridge payload: y, X, sparse Z, method, IDs, pedigree metadata -> HSquared.jl sparse engine -> compact result -> hsquared_fit object -> R summaries, extractors, plots, and diagnostics

title: Model status
text: R package scaffold CI. Team operating memory claim registers. hs_control() default validation-behavior experimental engine = "julia" option tiny local bridge examples. hs_control() stores planned CPU-thread, CUDA, AMDGPU, Metal, oneAPI backend names control metadata. backend_info() reports planned backend vocabulary marks backend execution unavailable. genomic(), single_step(), markers(), marker_scan(), qtl_scan() reserve planned formula vocabulary currently error implemented. animal() inert formula marker. v0.1 parser animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped). tested internal R--Julia payload shape y, X, sparse Z, method, family, encoded IDs, normalized pedigree metadata, Julia target metadata. experimental opt-control = hs_control(engine = "julia") path can send tiny payload sibling HSquared.jl checkout, marshal sparse Z CSC slots, normalize returned Julia result internal hsquared_fit contract. internal tiny validation fixture pins R payload ordering, sparse Z, live Julia pedigree_inverse() agreement three-animal pedigree local Julia bridge available. optional Mrode9/nadiv pedigree-Ainv comparator test nadiv sibling HSquared.jl checkout available. first fitted-object/extractor contract internal hsquared_fit objects mocked Julia result fields, including variance components, heritability, EBVs, PEV, reliability, fixed effects, random effects, log-likelihood, AIC, prediction, summaries. hs_data() lightweight input container ID maps phenotype, pedigree, genotype, expression, marker, annotation, environment inputs. Local tests accepted syntax, rejected future syntax, pedigree/data ID checks.

title: Model status
text: General model fitting. Default R--Julia bridge execution hsquared(). R-side Ainv construction. Real variance components, heritability, EBVs, BLUPs fitted models. PEV reliability current live Julia result payload. Full Mrode animal-model fit-output validation. ASReml, BLUPF90, DMU, WOMBAT comparator validation. File-backed genotype/omics loading streaming computation. Genomic single-step models. Multivariate factor-analytic G matrices. QTL-style effects, selfing, clonal inheritance, haplodiploidy, polyploidy, cytoplasmic inheritance, dominance, epistasis, GLLVM-style models. GPU execution.

title: Model status
text: long-term comparator set includes ASReml, MCMCglmm, sommer, BLUPF90, DMU, WOMBAT, JWAS.jl, XSim.jl, AGHmatrix, nadiv, drmTMB, gllvmTMB, DRM.jl, GLLVM.jl. Performance coverage claims evidence-gated. Public pages may call feature working code, tests, documentation, validation evidence exist.

what: R Interface for Julia-Backed Quantitative-Genetic Models
title: R Interface for Julia-Backed Quantitative-Genetic Models
text: hsquared planned R interface open, Julia-backed quantitative-genetic modelling system. R package owns applied-user surface: formula syntax, data validation, summaries, extractors, examples, eventually bridge HSquared.jl engine. repository moved past initial scaffold early Phase 1 parser bridge-contract work. can validate narrow v0.1 formula contract build tested internal R--Julia payload shape. default call validates stops, experimental opt-control = hs_control(engine = "julia") path can send tiny v0.1 payload sibling HSquared.jl checkout JuliaCall. still narrow local validation path, general animal-model support. fitted-object extractor contract now includes variance components, heritability, EBVs, PEV, reliability, fixed effects, random effects, log-likelihood, AIC, prediction, summaries, PEV/reliability waiting Julia result-payload fields. lightweight hs_data() container now records phenotype, pedigree, genotype, expression, marker, annotation, environment inputs future integrated workflows. intended two-package shape : first implementation target univariate Gaussian animal model: syntax parsed validated first contract. R side now constructs intended y, X, sparse Z, method, family, ID, normalized pedigree metadata payload. control = hs_control(engine = "julia"), internal tests can send sparse Z design Julia CSC slots, build Julia-side Ainv, run current validation target local sibling HSquared.jl checkout available. General public fitting waits production bridge validation-canon evidence. interface rule deliberately simple: easy, easy, easy. Applied users gold; package make common quantitative-genetic model feel obvious exposes specialist machinery.
code: hsquared       R package: friendly modelling interface for applied users HSquared.jl    Julia package: sparse quantitative-genetic engine fit <- hsquared(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = TRUE )

title: Create hsquared control options — hs_control
text: hs_control() records execution storage controls hsquared model calls. default engine = "validate" parses validates v0.1 contract stopping. experimental engine = "julia" path attempts current local Julia bridge tiny v0.1 animal-model payloads.

title: Create hsquared control options — hs_control
code: hs_control(   engine = c("validate", "julia"),   backend = c("auto", "cpu", "threads", "cuda", "amdgpu", "metal", "oneapi"),   accelerator = c("auto", "none", "gpu", "cuda", "amdgpu", "metal", "oneapi"),   precision = c("float64", "float32"),   save = c("minimal", "full", "tiny"),   engine_control = list() )

title: Create hsquared control options — hs_control
text: engine Execution engine. "validate" stops parser bridge payload validation. "julia" uses experimental local JuliaCall bridge. backend Planned compute backend. One "auto", "cpu", "threads", "cuda", "amdgpu", "metal", "oneapi". accelerator Planned accelerator preference. One "auto", "none", "gpu", "cuda", "amdgpu", "metal", "oneapi". precision Planned numeric precision. One "float64" "float32". save Planned fitted-object storage mode. One "minimal", "full", "tiny". engine_control named list engine-specific controls. current experimental Julia bridge recognizes julia_project initial.

title: Create hsquared control options — hs_control
text: object class "hs_control".

title: hsquared 0.0.0.9000
text: animal() now exported inert formula marker, hsquared() now parses narrow v0.1 formula contract animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) stopping planned Julia bridge boundary (#4, #6). hs_control() now experimental engine = "julia" option lets local developers run tiny v0.1 animal-model payload sibling HSquared.jl checkout via JuliaCall. default engine remains validation-, general fitted animal-model support still planned (#6). prediction_error_variance() reliability() now part R fitted-object extractor contract. work hsquared_fit objects containing result fields; current Julia bridge payload return yet (#5, #6). experimental Julia bridge now sends sparse Matrix::dgCMatrix random-effect designs Julia CSC slots instead densifying Z (#6). Added internal tiny animal-model validation fixture pins R payload ordering, sparse Z construction, live Julia pedigree_inverse() agreement three-animal Henderson-style pedigree sibling HSquared.jl checkout available (#7). Added optional Mrode9/nadiv pedigree-Ainv comparator fixture. nadiv sibling HSquared.jl checkout available, local tests compare Julia pedigree_inverse() nadiv::makeAinv() Mrode9 pedigree (#7). Expanded hs_control() preserve planned backend accelerator vocabulary CPU threads, CUDA, AMDGPU, Metal, oneAPI. control-surface placeholders ; GPU execution remains planned (#3). Added backend_info() users developers can inspect planned backend names seeing backend execution available yet. Added inert planned formula markers genomic(), single_step(), markers(), marker_scan(), qtl_scan(). parser now rejects terms explicit planned--implemented errors instead treating fixed effects. Added internal R--Julia bridge payload builder v0.1 animal-model contract. creates y, X, sparse Z, normalized pedigree metadata, validated Julia animal_model_spec() target used experimental Julia engine (#6). Added local-experimental JuliaCall smoke path tiny v0.1 payload sibling HSquared.jl checkout available. validates bridge shape Julia pedigree_inverse() fit_animal_model() yet public hsquared() fitting path (#6). Added first hsquared_fit object extractor contract, including variance_components(), heritability(), breeding_values(), fixef(), ranef(), logLik(), AIC(), predict(), summary() methods internal fit objects. contract plumbing Julia engine returns real fits (#5). Added hs_data() lightweight R data container phenotype, pedigree, genotype, marker, expression, annotation, environment inputs. records ID maps future integrated genomic/QTL/eQTL workflows, fit models (#8). Added Phase 0 project operating documentation, honest placeholder hsquared() entry point, hs_control() planned engine controls.
